Reprogramming   
![][image1]

Reprogram the charger over CANopen \- Revision 4 

(Firmware 3.1.0 and later)

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image3]**Reprogramming **2 of 10** 

**1 Reprogramming via CANopen** 

**1.1 Overview** 

The charger supports reprogramming its firmware, algorithms and configuration over the CANopen protocol. Repro gramming requires (1) a programming file with optional CRC from Delta-Q, (2) a programmer (i.e., CANopen device or PC-based tool that will transmit programming information to the charger), and (3) a charger. The programmer and charger should both be connected to a common CAN network. Other devices may be present on the network as long as they do not interfere with the reprogramming process. Otherwise, they should be powered-off or disconnected from the network. 

The reprogramming process involves several steps. The first step is an optional authentication step. If the charger is configured to require authentication then the programmer must perform this step before the remaining steps. Second, the programmer must notify the charger of its intent to reprogram it. This stops charging and puts the charger into programming mode. Third, the programmer must transfer the programming/configuration data (as provided by Delta-Q) to the charger. During this process, the charger will store and verify the transferred data. Finally, after completing the transfer, the charger will restart (i.e., CANopen communications will cease temporarily) and reprogram/reconfigure itself before starting up normally again and re-establishing communications. The programmer may query the charger to confirm that the charger has been successfully upgraded/reconfigured. The entire process is summarized in the diagram below. 

NOTE: For further information regarding configuration and download see document CANopen in Automation (CiA) 302: Additional Application Layer Functions Part 3: Configuration and Program Download. For details regarding SDO transfers see document CANopen in Automation (CiA) 301: CANopen Layer and Communication Profile. 

NOTE: If the charger responds with an SDO Abort Transfer at any point in the process then the reprogramming has failed and the programmer should not attempt to continue. The issue causing the failure should be diagnosed and corrected before another reprogramming attempt. 

![][image4]

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image5]**Reprogramming **3 of 10 ![][image6]**  
Figure 1.1: Reprogramming Sequence Diagram 

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image7]**Reprogramming **4 of 10** 

**1.2 Authentication (if required)** 

During the authentication step, the programmer requests a seed value from the charger, computes a key from the seed (see algorithm below) and transmits the key back to the charger. If the key matches the charger's key then the programmer will be authenticated. Otherwise, the charger will respond with an SDO Abort Transfer. If unsuccessful the programmer may retry the authentication. The programmer has five (5) seconds, after receiving the seed value, to transmit its key otherwise authentication will fail. If authentication fails more than three (3) times, either because of an invalid key or a timeout, the charger will be locked-out for thirty (30) seconds. Any attempts to authenticate during this period will be rejected regardless of the key's accuracy. 

The CANopen seed and key objects are summarized in the following table. 

| Name  | Object Index  | Sub-index  | Mode  | Data Type |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| Seed  | 0x2400  | 0x01  | Read-only  | Unsigned 32-bit, little-endian |
| Key  | 0x2400  | 0x02  | Write-only  | Unsigned 32-bit, little-endian |

A sample authentication trace is shown below. The charger has node address 10 (0xA). 

060A 8 40 00 24 01 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Upload Initiate \- Request (Seed)   
058A 8 43 00 24 01 46 CF 00 00 \- SDO Upload Initiate \- Response (Seed \= 0xCF46)   
060A 8 23 00 24 02 5A 00 00 00 \- SDO Download Initiate \- Request (Key \= 0x005A)   
058A 8 60 00 24 02 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Download Initiate \- Response (Confirmation) 

The key calculation algorithm (implemented in C) is shown below. The input start argument should be the result of adding the lower 16-bits of the seed (returned by an SDO read of 0x2400) and the customer-specific 16-bit secret (provided independently of this document). The result returned is the 16-bit key. 

static unsigned short sec\_CalcKey( unsigned short start )   
{   
short nandbit \= 0;   
short xorbit \= 0;   
short steps; 

union   
{   
struct   
{   
unsigned short \_0 : 1;   
unsigned short \_1 : 1;   
unsigned short \_2 : 1;   
unsigned short \_3 : 1;   
unsigned short \_4 : 1;   
unsigned short \_5 : 1;   
unsigned short \_6 : 1;   
unsigned short \_7 : 1;   
unsigned short \_8 : 1;   
unsigned short \_9 : 1;   
unsigned short \_10 : 1;   
unsigned short \_11 : 1;   
unsigned short \_12 : 1;   
unsigned short \_13 : 1;   
unsigned short \_14 : 1;   
unsigned short \_15 : 1;   
} bit; 

unsigned short word;   
} seed; 

seed.word \= start;   
for ( steps \= ( ( seed.bit.\_15 \* 8u ) \+ ( seed.bit.\_7 \* 4u ) \+ ( seed.bit.\_4 \* 2u ) \+ ( seed.bit.\_0 ) \+ 1u ); 

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image8]**Reprogramming **5 of 10** 

steps \> 0;   
steps-- )   
{   
nandbit \= \!( seed.bit.\_7 & seed.bit.\_2 );   
xorbit \= seed.bit.\_6 ^ seed.bit.\_9 ^ seed.bit.\_5 ^ seed.bit.\_0;   
seed.word \>\>= 1u;   
seed.bit.\_7 \= nandbit;   
seed.bit.\_10 \= xorbit;   
} 

return seed.word;   
} 

**1.3 Initiate** 

Per CiA 302, to initiate the reprogramming process, the programmer must command the charger to stop its normal operations and clear its existing firmware. The following CANopen object facilitates this process. 

| Name  | Object Index  | Sub-index  | Mode  | Data Type |
| :---: | ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| Program Control \- Command/Status  | 0x1F51  | 0x01  | Write/Read  | Unsigned 8-bit |

To ensure that the charger is stopped before reprogramming, the programmer should write a stop command (0x00) to 0x1F51:01 and then read 0x1F51:01 to confirm that the charger is stopped (0x00). If the charger is not stopped then the programmer should repeat the write and read operations. If, after three (3) attempts, the charger is not reporting stopped then the programmer should abandon the reprogramming operation. Try the entire reprogramming sequence again after powering-off the charger for thirty (30) seconds and powering it on again. 

Once the charger reports that it has stopped, the programmer should write a clear command (0x03) to 0x1F51:01. Now the charger is ready to accept the programming data block download. 

A sample initiation trace is shown below. The charger has node address 10 (0xA). 

060A 8 2F 51 1F 01 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Download Initiate \- Request (Stop Command) 058A 8 60 51 1F 01 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Download Initiate \- Response (Confirmation) 060A 8 40 51 1F 01 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Upload Initiate \- Request (Request Status) 058A 8 4F 51 1F 01 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Upload Initiate \- Response (Status \= Stopped) 060A 8 2F 51 1F 01 03 00 00 00 \- SDO Download Initiate \- Request (Clear Command) 058A 8 60 51 1F 01 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Download Initiate \- Response (Confirmation) 

**1.4 Block Download** 

Once the charger is ready to accept a block download, the programmer should proceed with an SDO Block Download Initiate per CiA 301\. The relevant CANopen object is shown in the table below. The programmer should set the data size indicator and specify the total size of the data file provided by Delta-Q. The data size field is an unsigned 32-bit, little-endian value. The charger supports the CANopen CRC. If the programmer supports the CANopen CRC then it should specify 1 in the "Client supports generating CRC on data" bit of the initiate message. The charger's response will indicate the expected sub-block size in segments where one (1) segment contains seven (7) bytes. The default (and maximum) sub-block size is 0x7F (127) segments or 127 x 7 \= 889 bytes, however, the charger may be configured for a smaller sub-block size if required (contact Delta-Q). 

| Name  | Object Index  | Sub-index  | Mode  | Data Type |
| :---: | ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| Program Data  | 0x1F50  | 0x01  | Write/Read  | Unsigned 8-bit |

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image9]**Reprogramming **6 of 10** 

To download all but the last sub-block, the programmer should transmit the expected number of SDO Block Download Sub-block segments and wait for the charger to confirm. If the confirmation's acknowledgement sequence number is zero then the programmer should retransmit the entire sub-block. If the acknowledgement sequence number is less than the number of segments transmitted by the programmer then the programmer should retransmit the missed segments. When the sub-block is successfully confirmed, the programmer should transmit the next sub-block. In the last segment of the last sub-block, the programmer should indicate, by setting the corresponding bit to one (1), that there are no more segments to transmit. The charger will respond to end the sub-block download. 

NOTE: After the programmer transmits the first sub-block, the charger may take as much as fifteen (15) seconds to confirm it. This is expected as the charger is erasing its non-volatile memory during this period. The response times for subsequent sub-blocks will be on the order of a few hundred milliseconds. 

A sample SDO Block Download Initiate trace is shown below. The charger has node address 10 (0xA). 

060A 8 C6 50 1F 01 8A BB 05 00 \- SDO Block Download Initiate \- Request   
(Indicates data size,   
total size \= 0x0005BB8A,   
supports CRC)   
058A 8 A4 50 1F 01 7F 00 00 00 \- SDO Block Download Initiate \- Response   
(Sub-block Size \= 0x7F seg,   
supports CRC) 

A sample SDO Block Download Sub-block trace is shown below. The charger has node address 10 (0xA). 

060A 8 01 32 00 01 00 04 00 FF \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Request (Data \= 0x32 ... 0xFF) 060A 8 02 FF FF FF FF FF FF FF \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Request (Data \= 0xFF ... 0xFF) 060A 8 03 FF FF FF FF FF FF FF \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Request (Data \= 0xFF ... 0xFF) . . . 

. . .   
. . .   
060A 8 7D 2D 28 5E 4C 2D 27 03 \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Request (Data \= 0x2D ... 0x03) 060A 8 7E 96 2D 28 5E 4C 2D 27 \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Request (Data \= 0x96 ... 0x27) 060A 8 7F 03 96 2D 28 5E 4C 2D \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Request (Data \= 0x03 ... 0x2D) 058A 8 A2 7F 7F 00 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Block Download Sub-block \- Response 

(Confirmation,   
acknowledgement sequence   
number \= 0x7F) 

After completing the sub-block downloads, the programmer should transmit an SDO Block Download End to complete the overall block download. If the programmer supports the CANopen CRC then it should be contained in the SDO Block Download End message. If the charger's CANopen CRC does not match then it will respond with an SDO Abort Transfer. Otherwise, the charger will respond with an SDO Block Download End confirmation. If the programmer does not support the CANopen CRC then it should transmit 0x0000. 

A sample SDO Block Download End trace is shown below. The charger has node address 10 (0xA). 

060A 8 C1 22 81 00 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Block Download End \- Request (CANopen CRC \= 0x8122) 058A 8 A1 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 \- SDO Block Download End \- Response (Confirmation) 

Within approximately three (3) seconds the charger will restart itself. During the restart, it will cease CAN commu nications for approximately ten (10) seconds while it's reprogramming/reconfiguring. When the charger's CANopen heartbeat returns the reprogramming process is complete. This marks the end of the required reprogramming steps. 

**1.4.1 Flash Programming Status (Optional)** 

During the block download process, the charger is reprogramming non-volatile flash memory. The programmer may query the charger periodically to determine the status of the reprogramming operations using CANopen object 0x1F57. 

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image10]**Reprogramming **7 of 10** 

| Name  | Object Index  | Sub-index  | Mode  | Data Type |
| :---: | ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| Flash Status  | 0x1F57  | 0x01  | Read-only  | Unsigned 32-bit |

0x1F57:01 is a bit field as described by the following table. 

| Bit(s)  | Value  | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 0  | 0  | Status OK, other bits are valid |
| \-  | 1  | In progress, other bits are not valid |
| 1 to 7  | 0  | No error occurred, valid program available |
| \-  | 1*∗*  | No valid program available |
| \-  | 2  | Data format unknown |
| \-  | 3  | Data format or CRC error |
| \-  | 4  | Flash not cleared before write |
| \-  | 5  | Flash write error |
| \-  | 6  | General address error |
| \-  | 7  | Flash secured |
| \-  | 8 to 62  | Reserved by CiA |
| \-  | 63  | Unspecified error |
| \-  | 64  | The program being downloaded does not support CAN reprogramming |
| \-  | 65  | The program being downloaded requires features not supported by the hardware |
| \-  | 66  | The program being downloaded requires unsupported features or is the wrong variant |
| \-  | 67 to 127  | Manufacturer-specific error codes for future use |
| 8 to 15  | 0  | Reserved by CiA, always 0 |
| 16 to 31  | 0  | Success. |
| \-  | 1  | Software not initialized. |
| \-  | 2  | Locked. Please make sure authentication is passed. |
| \-  | 3  | Ilegal function. |
| \-  | 4  | Download already in progress. |
| \-  | 5  | Script is already running. |
| \-  | 6  | Charge not stopped. |
| \-  | 7  | Write request has been seen. |
| \-  | 8  | Invalid sofware version. |
| \-  | 9  | Fail to unpack image header. |
| \-  | 10  | Invalid header file. |
| \-  | 11  | Invalid header size. |
| \-  | 12  | Invalid header type. |
| \-  | 13  | Invalid header CRC. |
| \-  | 14  | Error while erasing software. |
| \-  | 15  | Error while creating software image. |
| \-  | 16  | Can file invalid size. |
| \-  | 17  | Wrong image type. |
| \-  | 18  | Failed to write image. |
| \-  | 19  | Failed to verify image. |
| \-  | 20  | Too much data received. |
| \-  | 21  | Failed to write config file. |
| \-  | 22  | Failed to open config file. |
| \-  | 23  | Empty config file. |

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image11]**Reprogramming **8 of 10** 

| Bit(s)  | Value  | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| \-  | 24  | Too many algos. |
| \-  | 25  | Config item size error. |
| \-  | 26  | Can timeout error. |
| \-  | 27  | Server abort. |
| \-  | 28  | Software not compatible. |
| \-  | 29  | Invalid hardware version |
| \-  | 30  | Invalid object |
| \-  | 31  | Failed to read file. |
| \-  | 32  | Failed to unpack image header. |
| \-  | 33  | Failed to upgrade secondary application |

NOTE: *∗* This value (error code) is only applicable to algorithms 

| Group  | Error name  | Error value in  Object 0xIF57 | Potential cause  | Recommended  Actions |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Charger not ready/responding | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_NOT\_INIT, | 0x0001XXXX  | Not enough time be tween downloads or boot up | Allow enough time ( minimum 30 sec onds) between download or boot up. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_BUSY, | 0x0004XXXX  | Download already in progress. | Allow enough time ( minimum 30 sec onds) between download or boot up. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_SCRPT\_DL\_PR*←-* OG, | 0x0005XXXX  | Script is already run ning. | Allow enough time ( minimum 30 sec onds) between download or boot up. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CAN\_TM\_OUT, | 0x001AXXXX  | The communication beween the two de vice is not working. | Check CAN bus connection, bau drate and node IDs. |
| Configuration Error  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_LOCKED, | 0x0002XXXX  | Ilegal function  | Please contact DQT |
| Internal Error  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_ILL\_FUNC | 0x0003XXXX  | Bad state.Charge not stopped. | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_BAD\_STATE\_N*←-* O\_STOP | 0x0006XXXX  | Bad state. Write re quest has been sent but not working. | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_BAD\_STATE\_N*←-* O\_CLEAR | 0x0007XXXX  | Error while erasing software. | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_ERS\_FAIL | 0x000EXXXX  | Error while creating software image. | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CREATE\_FAIL | 0x000FXXXX  | Failed to verify im age. | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_VRFY\_FAIL | 0x0013XXXX  | Server abort.  | Please contact DQT |

**Confidential Information. \*Proprietary: This document is the property of Delta-Q Technologies Corp. Duplication of this document in whole or in part for any purposes other than those for which it was originally intended, without the written approval of Delta-Q Technologies or their agents, is strictly prohibited.**  
**![][image12]**Reprogramming **9 of 10** 

| Group  | Error name  | Error value in  Object 0xIF57 | Potential cause  | Recommended  Actions |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_SERVER\_ABORT | 0x001BXXXX  | Invalid object.  | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_OBJ | 0x001EXXXX  | Failed to upgrade secondary applica tion. | Please contact DQT |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_SEC\_APP\_BL\_F*←-* AIL | 0x0021XXXX  | Please make sure programming au thentication is passed. | Please contact DQT |
| File Error  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_SW\_VER | 0x0008XXXX  | The software dowloaded does not support Can*←-* Reprogramming. | Please try software version greater than 3.0.1. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_HDR\_TYPE | 0x000CXXXX  | The software down load has an invalid header type. | Make sure the downloaded soft ware matches the hardware it is downloaded to. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CREATE\_FAIL\_*←-* BLK\_SZ | 0x000BXXXX  | Can file header is in valid size. | Please make sure the size of the header file is less or equal 0x32. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CREATE\_FAIL\_*←-* BLK\_TYP | 0x000CXXXX  | Wrong image type.  | Please make sure your file contains the correct image type. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CFG\_ITEM\_FIL*←-* E\_WRTE | 0x0015XXXX  | Failed to write config file. | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CFG\_ITEM\_FIL*←-* E\_ERR | 0x0016XXXX  | Failed to open config file. | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CFG\_ITEM\_FIL*←-* E\_EMPTY | 0x0017XXXX  | Empty config file.  | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_TOO\_MANY\_AL*←-* GOS | 0x0018XXXX  | Too many algos.*←-* Only 25 or less algos are supported | Create a file wth 25 or less algos. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_CFG\_ITEM\_SZ\_*←-* ERR | 0x0019XXXX  | One or more DS items downloaded is of incorrect size. | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_SW\_FEAT | 0x001CXXXX  | Software not com patitble. | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_HW\_VER | 0x001DXXXX  | Invalid hardware version. | Use a newer hard ware version |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_WRAP | 0x0009XXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_WRAP\_CRC | 0x000AXXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_HDR\_SIZE | 0x000BXXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |

**![][image13]**Reprogramming **10 of 10** 

| Group  | Error name  | Error value in  Object 0xIF57 | Potential cause  | Recommended  Actions |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_INV\_HDR\_CRC | 0x000DXXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_READ\_FAIL | 0x001FXXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_UNPACK\_FAIL | 0x0020XXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |
| \-  | DQT\_SPEC\_ERR*←-* \_DATA\_OVERRUN | 0x0014XXXX  | Corrupted file  | Please contact DQT. |

• 

**1.5 Completion (Optional)** 

After the charger has reprogrammed/reconfigured itself and returned to the CANopen network, the programmer may query it for an overall Delta-Q CRC (provided by Delta-Q) to confirm that the reprogramming was successful. The CANopen object used to determine this is shown below. 

| Name  | Object Index  | Sub-index  | Mode  | Data Type |
| :---: | ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| Program Data  | 0x1F56  | 0x01  | Read-only  | Unsigned 8-bit |

If the CRC in the charger's response matches the one provided by Delta-Q then the charger contains the Delta-Q provided firmware, algorithms and configuration. 

A sample SDO Block Upload Initiate trace is shown below. The charger has node address 10 (0xA). 

060A 8 A4 56 1F 01 7F 20 00 00 \- SDO Block Upload Initiate \- Request   
(Block Size \= 0x7F segs,   
Expedite Threshold \= 0x20 bytes)   
058A 8 43 56 1F 01 3E C8 00 00 \- SDO Upload Initiate \- Response (CRC \= 0xC83E) **1.6 Revision History** 

| Rev.  | Date  | Author  | Description of Change |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1  | 18 Dec 2017  | PD  | Initial Draft |
| 2  | 5 Jul 2019  | ML  | Added more information for clarification |
| 3  | 22 Apr 2020  | TT  | Added Revision History Table. |
| 4  | 20 Oct 2020  | PD  | Added key algorithm. |
